description: Migrate Chrome anti-phishing extension from local GBM/ONNX inference to client-server architecture with DomURLs-BERT model via FastAPI, improving F1 from 87.27% to ~91.72% while reducing extension size from ~95MB to ~26MB.

userStories.title: Rewrite API request/response and remove RF logic
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to rewrite the FastAPI app to accept the new request format (URL + client_features) and return the new response format, removing all Random Forest / fallback logic.
acceptanceCriteria: PhishingRequest accepts { url: string, client_features: { length, dom_length, dot, hyphen, slash, at, params, shortened, tls, vowels_domain, email } }
PhishingResponse returns { url, is_phishing, confidence, label, analysis, inference_ms }
All RF logic removed (analyze_predictions, RF fields, sklearn imports)
Endpoint POST /predict functional
Endpoint GET /health returns model status, device (CPU/GPU), version
CORS middleware added (origins configurable, default *)
Tests with pytest pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: API lives in phishing-api/ directory. Remove all Random Forest / fallback logic.

userStories.title: Fix model input text format to match training
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to fix the create_feature_text function to match the training format, fixing the critical bug where the current format doesn't match what the model was trained on.
acceptanceCriteria: Text follows format: [URL] <url> [AGE] <dias>d [REG] <registrar> [EXPIRE] <dias>d [WHOIS] found|not_found [EXTRA] redirects=N tls=N dot=N ...
Tokenizer max_length corrected from 512 to 128
When WHOIS not available, uses [WHOIS] not_found and default values for age/reg/expire
Feature domain_google_index removed (fixed value -1 or omitted from text)
Unit test validates generated text format against training dataset examples
Tests with pytest pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Critical bug fix - model performance depends on matching training format exactly.

userStories.title: Add server-side feature extraction (WHOIS + DNS)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the API to extract server-side features (WHOIS data, DNS records, redirects) that cannot be obtained from the browser extension.
acceptanceCriteria: WHOIS lookup with local cache (whois_cache.json), live fallback with 3s timeout
DNS queries: MX servers, nameservers, SPF record, A record - 2s timeout each
HTTP redirect chain counting
Features extracted: redirects, dom_age, dom_expire, mx_servers, nameservers, dom_spf, dom_in_ip, srv_client
domain_google_index returns fixed -1 (feature removed)
Failure in any individual lookup does not block response (uses default -1)
Latency p95 < 500ms with cached WHOIS
Tests with pytest pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use asyncio.gather to parallelize WHOIS/DNS lookups.

userStories.title: Add batch prediction endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a batch prediction endpoint for efficiency when multiple URLs need analysis.
acceptanceCriteria: Endpoint POST /predict-batch accepts array of requests
Returns array of responses in same order
Tokenization in batch (not N sequential model calls)
Test with batch of 5 URLs works correctly
Tests with pytest pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Dockerize the API
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the API deployable as a Docker container for easy deployment.
acceptanceCriteria: Dockerfile functional with dependencies (torch, transformers, fastapi, whois, dnspython)
docker-compose.yml with port and volume configuration for model
Container starts and responds on /health without errors
Minimal documentation on how to start (docker-compose up)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create clientFeatures.ts module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a new TypeScript module that extracts the 11 client-side features from a URL, replacing the old features.ts (51 features do GBM).
acceptanceCriteria: File src/utils/clientFeatures.ts created
Extracts all 11 features: length, dom_length, dot, hyphen, slash, at, params, shortened, tls, vowels_domain, email
shortened checks against list of known shorteners (bit.ly, tinyurl, t.co, etc.)
email uses regex to detect email in URL
Exported function: extractClientFeatures(url: string): ClientFeatures
Interface ClientFeatures exported with 11 fields typed as number
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create api.ts HTTP client module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an API client module for the extension to communicate with the FastAPI backend.
acceptanceCriteria: File src/utils/api.ts created
Function analyzeUrl(url: string, features: ClientFeatures): Promise<ApiResponse> exported
Function checkHealth(): Promise<HealthResponse> exported
5-second timeout on all calls
API URL configurable via chrome.storage.sync (default: http://localhost:8000)
Network errors return { offline: true } instead of throw
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create cache.ts module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a URL analysis cache to avoid repeated API calls for frequently visited URLs.
acceptanceCriteria: File src/utils/cache.ts created
Interface CacheEntry: { isPhishing, confidence, label, analysis, timestamp }
Storage: chrome.storage.local under key urlCache
Lookup key: normalized URL (lowercase hostname, no trailing slash)
TTL: 24h for LEGITIMATE, 7 days for PHISHING
Limit: 5000 entries; when exceeded, remove 20% oldest (LRU eviction)
Exported functions: getCached(url), setCached(url, entry), clearCache()
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Rewrite background.ts orchestrator
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to rewrite the background service worker to orchestrate the analysis pipeline: whitelist -> blacklist -> cache -> API, replacing the current ONNX-based flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Analysis pipeline: whitelist (instant) -> blacklist (instant) -> cache (instant) -> API call
Result stored per tab in chrome.storage.session
Messages supported: ANALYZE_URL, GET_RESULT, GET_API_STATUS, CLEAR_CACHE, SET_API_URL
ANALYZE_URL receives { url, features } and returns { isPhishing, confidence, label, analysis, source, offline? }
source indicates origin: blacklist | whitelist | cache | api | offline
API failure returns { offline: true } (fail-open, no blocking)
Service Worker restarts without cache loss (cache in storage.local, not in-memory)
No references to ONNX, inference.ts, or features.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update detector.ts content script
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to update the content script to extract client features, send them to the background, and display banners based on API results.
acceptanceCriteria: Ignores internal pages (chrome://, about://, edge://)
Extracts 11 client_features using clientFeatures.ts
Sends { type: ANALYZE_URL, url, features } to background
Phishing detected (any source) -> red banner
Confidence < 70% -> orange banner (warning)
Legitimate -> no banner
Offline -> no banner (fail-open)
No references to inference.ts or old features.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Rewrite popup.tsx
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the analysis result, API status, and have controls to manage cache and API settings when I click the extension icon.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove all ONNX model loading
Gets result from background via GET_RESULT message
Shows card with: result (PHISHING/LEGITIMATE), confidence (%), analysis text, source
Visual indicator for API online/offline (checks via GET_API_STATUS)
Functional Clear Cache button (calls CLEAR_CACHE)
Configurable API URL field (calls SET_API_URL)
Text indicates DomURLs-BERT via API as analysis engine
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update logger.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to update the logger to reflect the new analysis sources.
acceptanceCriteria: Field source added: blacklist | whitelist | cache | api | offline
Field featuresUsed removed (no longer relevant)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Clean package.json and assets
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to remove ONNX/WASM dependencies and assets that are no longer needed.
acceptanceCriteria: onnxruntime-web removed from package.json
tldts removed from package.json (if not used elsewhere)
wasm-unsafe-eval removed from CSP in manifest
web_accessible_resources cleaned (remove .onnx and .wasm references)
Files deleted: src/utils/inference.ts, src/utils/features.ts
Assets deleted: assets/modelo/modelo_phishing_ort14.onnx, assets/ort*.wasm, assets/ort.min.js, assets/ort.wasm.min.js
npm install runs without errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Be careful not to delete files that are still referenced. Do this after all other extension stories are done.

userStories.title: End-to-end integration tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify the full pipeline works: extension <-> API with all scenarios.
acceptanceCriteria: API starts via docker-compose up without errors
Extension loads in Chrome without console errors
Scenario: blacklist hit -> instant red banner, no API call
Scenario: whitelist hit -> no banner, no API call
Scenario: unknown URL -> API call, correct result, cached
Scenario: same URL again -> result from cache, no API call
Scenario: API offline -> fail-open, popup shows offline
Scenario: API back online -> next unknown URL uses API normally
Popup shows correct result for each scenario
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the final validation story. All previous stories must pass first.
